doc: Dispatch-Class Taxonomy (epic #1669, slice #1670). The single machine-readable table that owns the autopilot dispatch-class alphabet: one row per class. decide.py derives PIPELINE_SLOTS / SIGNAL_CLASSES / SIGNAL_COOLDOWNS from this file at import time (fail-loud, no fallback tuples); src/taxonomy/classes.ts re-exports the same rows as typed views for TS call sites. Row order is significant: it IS the slot/signal dispatch order. Columns: name, kind (pipeline|signal), skill (the Claude Code skill the class dispatches), costClass (src/metrics/aggregate.ts CostClass bucket), learningAgent (planner|executor|null — which pattern-memory agent the class's lessons train), cooldownSeconds (signal classes only; null for pipeline slots), scope (orch|target|both), provenanceLabel (the GitHub label the class's filing skill stamps; null for classes that file nothing). Nullable columns are explicitly null, never absent. POLICY stays in decide.py (selectors, scope masks, BACKFILL_SIGNAL_CLASSES stagger set, cost-cap gates) — this table is only the ALPHABET (ADR-0012).

classes.name: dev_orch
classes.notes: Implements ready-for-agent orchestrator issues in a fresh worktree and opens a PR. Verification failures train the executor pattern memory.

classes.name: qa_orch
classes.notes: Reviews orchestrator PRs (Standards + Spec sub-agents). QA failures train the planner pattern memory (the missed criterion).

classes.name: research_orch
classes.notes: Board-level orchestrator research. The playbook also routes per-issue enrichment to hydra-issue-research; hydra-research is the canonical class skill.

classes.name: dev_target
classes.notes: Full Target (~/hydra-betting) build cycle. Verification failures train the executor pattern memory. Per-cycle USD backstop: issue #1059.

classes.name: qa_target
classes.notes: Independent Target PR review (money-critical Sonnet floor per the model-routing map). The autopilot playbook's older 'hydra-qa (target scope)' phrasing predates the dedicated hydra-target-qa playbook. NOTE for slice #1671: today's VALID_SKILLS in src/pattern-memory/subagent-capture.ts omits hydra-target-qa, so its planner lessons are not yet captured — folding that projection onto this row is an intentional decision point.

classes.name: research_target
classes.notes: Full Target research cycle: vision, grounding, priorities.md/roadmap.md, work-queue items.

classes.name: design_concept_orch
classes.notes: issue #466, Phase B of #437: grills an orchestrator anchor before its dev_orch dispatch. Phase B is warn-only — a draft artifact whose gateCheck() returns ok:false is still treated as 'fresh artifact present' and dev_orch proceeds. Phase C (separate issue) will flip this to a hard block. The target mirror (design_concept_target) lands in Phase D.

classes.name: health
classes.notes: Whole-system health probe. Always allowed — cooldown 0; rate-limited by the signal itself. The only scope-agnostic class: never excluded by the orch-only/target-only scope masks.

classes.name: sweep_orch
classes.notes: Orchestrator issue-board sweep; 15 min cooldown.

classes.name: sweep_target
classes.notes: Target backlog sweep; 15 min cooldown.

classes.name: discover_orch
classes.notes: issue #959, epic #958: 1h backfill cadence. Dropped from 30m to 3600s so it round-robins with architecture_orch off the unified orch_backfill_idle signal — both backfill-set classes share the same 1h cadence and the one-per-turn stagger guard (BACKFILL_SIGNAL_CLASSES in decide.py) picks one per idle turn. Files issues via the standard triage flow; stamps no dedicated provenance label.

classes.name: discover_target
classes.notes: Runtime diagnostic discovery on the Target; 30 min cooldown.

classes.name: scout_orch
classes.notes: issue #485, Phase B of /hydra-tool-scout epic: weekly calendar walk over the AI-leverage taxonomy + runtime deps (src/scout/calendar-walk.ts). 7d class cooldown: the walk takes ~a week's worth of context to digest (10 categories + 2 dep manifests ≈ 12 dispatches; running it more often produces duplicate work because the per-category cooldown is 30d). Per-class cooldown is the back-stop; per-category (30d) + per-tool (90d seen-list) cooldowns are the primary suppressors, and the issue #532 cost-cap gate fires before the cooldown.

classes.name: architecture_orch
classes.notes: issue #790, parent #787; unified by #959, epic #958: board-idle backfill that dispatches the headless /hydra-architecture-scan wrapper (#788) to turn spare capacity into self-improvement issues. Fires when orch_backfill_idle AND NOT arch_board_saturated; arch_board_saturated is the PRIMARY suppressor (checked FIRST in the selector) and matters MORE at hourly cadence; the 1h class cooldown is only the back-stop. In BACKFILL_SIGNAL_CLASSES (one-per-turn stagger). collect-state.sh owns signal emission; decide.py only reads the precomputed signals.

classes.name: retro_orch
classes.notes: issue #920, parent #917: daily per-run retrospective. Dispatches /hydra-retro (#919) to turn the most-recent COMPLETED run into conservative, recurrence-gated improvement proposals (≤2 issues, ≤1 gated PR — stamps no dedicated provenance label). Fires on retro_run_available (collect-state.sh emits it when a completed run exists); the 24h class cooldown enforces the once-per-day cadence so the same run isn't re-analysed every idle turn. Signal classes dispatch AFTER pipeline slots, so a retro never preempts a dev/QA/research dispatch. NOT in BACKFILL_SIGNAL_CLASSES (run-anchored, 24h).

classes.name: cleanup_orch
classes.notes: issue #960, parent #958: board-idle backfill dispatching the headless /hydra-cleanup skill — a DETERMINISTIC dead-code + simplification detector (knip) that files high-confidence, mechanically-verifiable findings ('remove X AND npm test/tsc still pass') as ready-for-agent issues. Keyed off the same unified orch_backfill_idle signal as architecture_orch/discover_orch (#959) with a 1h cooldown, but DELIBERATELY NOT in BACKFILL_SIGNAL_CLASSES — cleanup is the high-confidence mechanical workhorse and is meant to 'run hot' alongside the staggered backfill set. Its anti-flood cap is cleanup_board_saturated, checked FIRST in the selector.

classes.name: cleanup_target
classes.notes: The TARGET mirror of cleanup_orch — dispatches the headless /hydra-target-cleanup skill, a DETERMINISTIC demote-only dead-export sweep over ~/hydra-betting/web (knip is a Target devDependency since hydra-betting PR #93). Files Redis backlog items (the Target's tracker), NOT GitHub issues — hence provenanceLabel null — and emits ONLY demote-class findings in files past the Target's 45-day wiring grace period (the carve-out the Target's CLAUDE.md rule 3 authorises). Keyed off target_backfill_idle with target_cleanup_board_saturated as the anti-flood cap checked FIRST, mirroring cleanup_orch's signal discipline exactly. Same 1h cadence as cleanup_orch. NOTE for slice #1671: today's skillToCostClass in src/metrics/aggregate.ts omits hydra-target-cleanup (buckets to 'other'); this row records the intended 'cleanup' attribution — folding that projection onto this row is an intentional decision point.

classes.name: wire_or_retire_target
classes.notes: issue #2722, epic #2720: the judgment counterpart to cleanup_target's mechanical sweep. cleanup_target files needs-triage `wire-or-retire`-labelled Redis backlog items for Target modules past the 45-day wiring grace; those items are the DECISION queue, and the prompt-shaped resolver protocol drafted in their bodies is what failed (items were laundered into the backlog lane — hence the #2721 lane guard). This class dispatches the headless /hydra-wire-or-retire skill to actually RESOLVE those items: git-log archaeology + cross-ref of config/direction vision/priorities/roadmap + the Target backlog open AND done lanes -> a WIRE / RETIRE / UNCLEAR verdict per module (rewrite the item into a concrete ready-for-agent wiring/retirement task, or route ready-for-human on UNCLEAR). Hard carve-out: modules under web/src/lib/risk/ or live-execution paths ALWAYS route ready-for-human (interim hardcoded list until #2701 classifyTargetRisk exists). Ambiguity never resolves to deletion (Target CLAUDE.md rule 6). Fires on the `wire_or_retire_target_available` signal (collect-state.sh emits it when >=1 open wire-or-retire-labelled item sits in the Target triage lane); resolves at most 2 items per run. 24h class cooldown (SIGNAL_COOLDOWNS) enforces the once-per-day cadence and MUST be seeded in bootstrap.sh signal_last_fired so it survives the pace-gate relaunch (the #2575 retro-cooldown bootstrap bug class). Target-scope, so orch-only excludes it (mirror of cleanup_target). NOT in BACKFILL_SIGNAL_CLASSES. Dispatch OMITS the model param (inherit parent per #1093 — judgment work; the Haiku-premature-exit failure mode is documented).

classes.name: design_qa_target
classes.notes: issue #2739, parent #2732 (Target UI-quality loop). Periodic visual QA of the Target's UI: dispatches the headless /hydra-design-qa skill (7d class cooldown, mirroring scout_orch's weekly calendar discipline) to capture the slice-1 screenshot set of every nav-registry route on ~/hydra-betting/web, judge each page against the Target design-language ADR's judgment rules (hydra-betting/docs/adr/0005-design-language.md — density budget, clutter, consistency), and file AT MOST 3 deduped needs-triage Target-backlog items per run, each citing the specific ADR rule violated + screenshot evidence. JUDGMENT work, so findings route needs-triage (NOT ready-for-agent), mirroring wire_or_retire_target's confidence-routing discipline (epic #2720). Fires on `design_qa_target_due` (collect-state.sh emits it true whenever the Target board is reachable AND not saturated); the 7d SIGNAL_COOLDOWNS cadence is the calendar back-stop and MUST be seeded in bootstrap.sh signal_last_fired so it survives the pace-gate relaunch (the #2575 cooldown-bootstrap bug class). `design_qa_target_saturated` is the anti-flood cap checked FIRST — skip when >5 open `design-qa`-labelled Target-backlog items already await triage. Target-scope, so orch-only excludes it (mirror of cleanup_target / wire_or_retire_target). NOT in BACKFILL_SIGNAL_CLASSES. Dispatch OMITS the model param (inherit parent per #1093 — judgment work; the Haiku-premature-exit failure mode is documented).

classes.name: skill_prune
classes.notes: issue #2949, epic #2944 (skill-quality overhaul). The recurring, eval-gated PROMPT counterpart to cleanup_orch's mechanical dead-CODE sweep: dispatches the headless /hydra-skill-prune skill to prune the Orchestrator's playbook-generated skills. Each run picks exactly ONE generated skill (largest-over-baseline first, else round-robin) and proposes deletions along the Pocock pruning taxonomy (duplication / sediment / no-op). The deletion test is made deterministic — candidates are validated by running the promptfoo eval (npm run eval --config evals/skill-prune.yaml, offline echo provider) and requiring golden-task contract-token parity before a PR opens; a failing eval aborts the PR and files a needs-triage issue listing the candidates instead. Output is at most one T1/T2 PR per run editing only that playbook (plus its regenerated skill and its shrink-only-tightened scripts/ci/skill-size-baseline.json entry). 7d class cooldown (SIGNAL_COOLDOWNS), the low-cadence scout_orch calendar discipline — the accretion worth pruning takes a week to accumulate. MUST be seeded in bootstrap.sh signal_last_fired so it survives the pace-gate relaunch (the #2575 retro/arch/cleanup/scout cooldown-bootstrap bug class). Fires on `orch_backfill_idle` (spare-capacity backfill, same signal as architecture_orch/cleanup_orch) gated by `skill_prune_board_saturated` as the anti-flood cap checked FIRST. NOT in BACKFILL_SIGNAL_CLASSES — like cleanup_orch it rides the idle signal but rate-limits on its own 7d cooldown, not the one-per-turn stagger. Orch-scope, so target-only excludes it (mirror of cleanup_orch / scout_orch / architecture_orch). Dispatch stamps apply:true (the #1078 anti-dry-run-no-op lesson — the skill is dry-run by default) and OMITS the model param (inherit parent per #1093 — judgment work).
